an1.140:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who illuminate non-*dhamma* as non-*dhamma* — those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, are practicing for the welfare of the many, for the happiness of the many, for the benefit, welfare, and happiness of the many, of gods and humans.
an1.140:1.2: And those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, generate much merit, and they uphold this true *dhamma*."
an1.141:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who illuminate *dhamma* as *dhamma* — those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, are practicing for the welfare of the many, for the happiness of the many, for the benefit, welfare, and happiness of the many, of gods and humans.
an1.141:1.2: And those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, generate much merit, and they uphold this true *dhamma*."
an1.142-149:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who illuminate non-*vinaya* as non-*vinaya* … who illuminate *vinaya* as *vinaya* … who illuminate what was not spoken or uttered by the Tathāgata as not spoken or uttered by the Tathāgata … who illuminate what was spoken and uttered by the Tathāgata as spoken and uttered by the Tathāgata … who illuminate what was not practiced by the Tathāgata as not practiced by the Tathāgata … who illuminate what was practiced by the Tathāgata as practiced by the Tathāgata … who illuminate what was not prescribed by the Tathāgata as not prescribed by the Tathāgata … who illuminate what was prescribed by the Tathāgata as prescribed by the Tathāgata;
an1.142-149:1.2: those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, are practicing for the welfare of the many, for the happiness of the many, for the benefit, welfare, and happiness of the many, of gods and humans.
an1.142-149:1.3: And those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, generate much merit, and they uphold this true *dhamma*."
